title: ASML reports €9.3 billion total net sales and €2.9 billion net income in Q2 2026
gist: ASML posted Q2 2026 net sales of €9.3B and net income of €2.9B, both above its own guidance, and raised full-year 2026 sales guidance to €43-45B while laying out concrete plans to expand EUV and DUV tool capacity roughly 30% for 2027 with further expansion under review for 2028.

insights.claim: ASML reported Q2 2026 total net sales of €9.3 billion, gross margin of 54.0%, and net income of €2.9 billion, with both sales and margin coming in above the company's own guidance, driven primarily by higher-than-expected Installed Base Management sales.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: A beat-and-raise quarter signals AI-driven lithography demand is running ahead of ASML's own expectations, supportive for ASML shares and the broader WFE supply chain.
insights.quote: Our second-quarter total net sales were €9.3 billion and gross margin came in at 54.0%, both above guidance, driven primarily by higher than expected Installed Base Management sales.

insights.claim: ASML guided Q3 2026 total net sales to between €11.0 billion and €12.0 billion with gross margin between 55% and 57%, implying a sharp sequential jump from Q2's €9.3 billion.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: A roughly 20-30% QoQ sales guidance step-up is a strong forward signal of accelerating EUV/DUV shipments into H2 2026.
insights.quote: ASML expects Q3 2026 total net sales between €11.0 billion and €12.0 billion, and a gross margin between 55% and 57%

insights.claim: ASML raised its full-year 2026 total net sales guidance to a range of €43 billion to €45 billion with gross margin of 54-56%.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: An upward full-year guidance revision confirms AI capex is flowing through into lithography orders, a leading indicator for the broader foundry capex cycle.
insights.quote: ASML now expects 2026 total net sales to be between €43 billion and €45 billion, with a gross margin between 54% and 56%

insights.claim: CEO Christophe Fouquet said ongoing AI-related investment and continued progress in AI technology are driving demand for advanced Logic and Memory chips, which is strengthening customers' capacity-expansion commitments and giving ASML increased visibility into longer-term demand, with order intake remaining extremely strong in H1 2026.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Management explicitly links AI capex to durable multi-year order visibility, supporting a longer WFE upcycle thesis rather than a short-term demand pull-forward.
insights.quote: Ongoing AI-related investments and continued progress in AI technologies are driving demand for advanced Logic and Memory chips, further strengthening the semiconductor industry's growth outlook.

insights.claim: ASML plans to add 30% to its 2026 low-NA EUV capacity of around 65 systems for 2027, and is investigating a further 30% increase for 2028; it is planning a similar 30% capacity add to its 2026 DUV immersion base of around 130 systems for 2027, with another possible 30% increase under investigation for 2028.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Concrete unit-capacity expansion targets (roughly +20 EUV and +40 DUV systems per year) are a hard input for modeling TSMC/Samsung/Intel/China capex absorption and ASML's equipment revenue run-rate into 2028.
insights.quote: we are planning to add 30% to our 2026 low NA EUV capacity of around 65 for 2027, and we are investigating to increase capacity with another 30% for 2028
